name: feat-messaging-circuit-breaker-and-retry-backpressure
title: Add circuit breaker and adaptive retry backpressure to messaging
description: Per-consumer-group circuit breaker that pauses transport consumers on sustained transient failures, plus adaptive retry processor polling that backs off under high failure rates. Reduces DB pressure during downstream outages.

userStories.title: Add ICircuitBreakerStateManager and circuit state types [M]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Create the core circuit breaker state machine as an internal service. Includes CircuitState enum (Closed/Open/HalfOpen), per-group state tracking via ConcurrentDictionary, ReportFailure/ReportSuccess methods, IsTransientException predicate evaluation, escalating open duration, and thread-safe state transitions. Ship CircuitBreakerDefaults.IsTransient as the default predicate.
acceptanceCriteria: CircuitState transitions correctly: Closed→Open after N transient failures, Open→HalfOpen after duration, HalfOpen→Closed on success, HalfOpen→Open on transient failure
Only exceptions where IsTransientException predicate returns true increment failure counter; non-matching exceptions are ignored
Consecutive failure counter resets to 0 on any success
Open duration escalates on repeated reopens (30s→60s→120s→240s) and resets after 3 healthy cycles
Thread-safe: concurrent ReportFailure/ReportSuccess calls do not corrupt state
Non-transient probe failure (HalfOpen) transitions to Closed, not Open

userStories.title: Add CircuitBreakerOptions and ConsumerCircuitBreakerOptions config [S]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Add configuration types for global and per-consumer-group circuit breaker settings. Wire into MessagingOptions. Add WithCircuitBreaker() to IConsumerBuilder<T>.
acceptanceCriteria: CircuitBreakerOptions has FailureThreshold (default 5), OpenDuration (30s), MaxOpenDuration (4min), HalfOpenProbeCount (1), IsTransientException predicate (default: CircuitBreakerDefaults.IsTransient)
ConsumerCircuitBreakerOptions has Enabled (default true), FailureThreshold, OpenDuration, IsTransientException predicate (composable with CircuitBreakerDefaults.IsTransient)
WithCircuitBreaker(cb => ...) fluent API works on IConsumerBuilder<T>
Configuration validation rejects invalid values (threshold=0, maxOpen < open, null predicate)

userStories.title: Add PauseAsync/ResumeAsync to IConsumerClient [S]
userStories.description:
Sequential: add interface methods → add default implementations → verify compile.

Extend IConsumerClient with PauseAsync and ResumeAsync. Both are idempotent. Default implementation is no-op (backward compatible for custom transports).
acceptanceCriteria: IConsumerClient has PauseAsync(CancellationToken) and ResumeAsync(CancellationToken) methods
Default interface implementations are no-op (ValueTask.CompletedTask) for backward compatibility
XML docs specify idempotency contract and in-flight message behavior

userStories.title: Refactor ConsumerRegister for per-group CTS and client tracking [L]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Refactor ConsumerRegister.ExecuteAsync to create per-group CancellationTokenSource and track IConsumerClient instances per group. Replace fire-and-forget tasks with tracked GroupHandle objects. Maintain existing behavior — no circuit breaker logic yet, just the infrastructure for per-group pause/resume.
acceptanceCriteria: Each consumer group has its own CancellationTokenSource (not shared global _cts)
GroupHandle tracks CTS, client list, and original concurrency per group
Existing PulseAsync/ReStartAsync behavior preserved (cancels all groups, restarts all)
_isHealthy global boolean kept intact for TransportCheckProcessor compatibility
All existing messaging tests pass without modification

userStories.title: Integrate circuit breaker into ConsumerRegister (pause/resume on state change) [M]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Connect ICircuitBreakerStateManager to ConsumerRegister. When circuit opens: cancel group CTS + PauseAsync on clients. When half-open timer fires: drain in-flight, resume with concurrency=1. On close: restore original concurrency.
acceptanceCriteria: Circuit open → group's transport consumers paused, no new messages pulled
Half-open → drains in-flight, resumes with concurrency=1, admits 1 probe message
Circuit close → original concurrency restored
TransportCheckProcessor.ReStartAsync does NOT reset circuit state
Per-group pause does not affect other groups' consumers

userStories.title: Handle TaskCanceledException timeout in SubscribeExecutor._InvokeConsumerMethodAsync [S]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

MUST precede US-007. The OperationCanceledException catch in _InvokeConsumerMethodAsync currently swallows all cancellations unconditionally. Timeout-sourced TaskCanceledExceptions (HttpClient timeouts) never reach _SetFailedState and are invisible to the circuit breaker. Fix: re-throw TaskCanceledException when inner CancellationToken.IsCancellationRequested is false (timeout, not shutdown). App-shutdown cancellations continue to be swallowed.
acceptanceCriteria: TaskCanceledException where CancellationToken.IsCancellationRequested is false is re-thrown (not swallowed)
OperationCanceledException from app shutdown continues to be swallowed (existing behavior)
HttpClient timeout scenario propagates to _SetFailedState and becomes visible to circuit breaker

userStories.title: Wire SubscribeExecutor failure reporting to ICircuitBreakerStateManager [M]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Depends on US-006 (TaskCanceledException fix). Inject ICircuitBreakerStateManager into SubscribeExecutor. Call ReportFailureAsync on handler exceptions, ReportSuccess on handler completion. Group name resolved from ConsumerExecutorDescriptor.
acceptanceCriteria: Handler exceptions trigger ReportFailureAsync(groupName, exception) on the state manager
Handler success triggers ReportSuccess(groupName)
Group name resolved from ConsumerExecutorDescriptor.Attribute.Group
Existing failure persistence (DB status, retry count) unchanged

userStories.title: Implement PauseAsync/ResumeAsync for RabbitMQ transport [M]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Implement PauseAsync via BasicCancelAsync(consumerTag) — stops new message deliveries; in-flight OnMessageCallback invocations are fire-and-forget and allowed to complete naturally (do NOT close/dispose the channel during pause). ResumeAsync re-registers via BasicConsumeAsync. CTS cancellation is NOT a safe pause mechanism for RabbitMQ — it exits the ListeningAsync loop but leaves in-flight callbacks running detached; broker redelivers on channel close. PauseAsync (not CTS) is the primary pause path.
acceptanceCriteria: PauseAsync calls BasicCancelAsync(consumerTag) to stop new message deliveries
PauseAsync does NOT close the channel — in-flight callbacks complete naturally
ResumeAsync re-registers consumer with BasicConsumeAsync
Both methods are idempotent
Integration test: pause stops new message delivery, in-flight messages complete, resume restarts delivery

userStories.title: Implement PauseAsync/ResumeAsync for Kafka transport [M]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Implement PauseAsync via consumer.Pause(partitions). ResumeAsync via consumer.Resume(partitions). Native Kafka support.
acceptanceCriteria: PauseAsync calls consumer.Pause on assigned partitions
ResumeAsync calls consumer.Resume on paused partitions
Both methods are idempotent
Integration test: pause stops consumption, resume restarts it

userStories.title: Implement PauseAsync/ResumeAsync for remaining transports [L]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Implement PauseAsync/ResumeAsync for AWS SQS (stop polling), Azure Service Bus (StopProcessingAsync), Redis Streams (stop XREADGROUP), NATS (Unsubscribe), Pulsar (PauseAsync native), InMemory (stop dequeue). InMemory is critical for unit testing.
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 transports implement idempotent PauseAsync/ResumeAsync
InMemory transport supports pause/resume (enables circuit breaker testing without Docker)
Each transport's pause mechanism stops new message delivery
Each transport's resume mechanism restarts delivery

userStories.title: Add adaptive polling to MessageNeedToRetryProcessor [M]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Replace fixed _waitingInterval with adaptive logic. Track transient failure rate per cycle. Double interval on >80% transient, halve after 2 healthy cycles, reset after 3 clean cycles.
acceptanceCriteria: Polling interval doubles when >80% of executed batch are transient failures
Interval caps at MaxPollingInterval (default 15min)
Interval halves after 2 consecutive healthy cycles (>50% success)
Interval resets to base after 3 consecutive cycles with 0 transient failures
AdaptivePolling can be disabled via config (falls back to fixed interval)

userStories.title: Add retry processor circuit-state awareness [S]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Retry processor checks ICircuitBreakerStateManager before re-enqueuing. Messages for groups with open circuits are skipped. Skipped messages excluded from adaptive polling rate calculation.
acceptanceCriteria: Messages for groups with open circuits are not re-enqueued
Skipped-due-to-open-circuit messages excluded from adaptive polling rate
When circuit closes, retry processor resumes normal behavior for that group

userStories.title: Add OpenTelemetry metrics and logging for circuit breaker [M]
userStories.description:
Sequential: add counters/gauges → add logging → verify.

Add OTel metrics: circuit trip counter, open duration histogram, state gauge per group. Log state transitions at Warning level. Integrate with existing Messaging.OpenTelemetry package.
acceptanceCriteria: OTel counter for circuit trips (tagged by group name)
OTel histogram for open duration
OTel gauge for current circuit state per group
State transitions logged at Warning level with group name, old state, new state, and failure count

userStories.title: Add configuration validation for circuit breaker options [S]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS.

Validate circuit breaker config at startup. Reject invalid values with clear error messages.
acceptanceCriteria: FailureThreshold = 0 throws ArgumentOutOfRangeException at startup
MaxOpenDuration < OpenDuration throws ArgumentException
Null IsTransientException predicate throws ArgumentNullException at startup
CircuitBreakerDefaults.IsTransient is documented as composable (users chain via || operator)

userStories.title: Update docs and package READMEs [S]
userStories.description:
Sequential: update llms/messaging.txt → update Core README → update Abstractions README → verify.

Document circuit breaker configuration, failure classification, adaptive retry, and cluster scope limitations.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/llms/messaging.txt has circuit breaker section with configuration examples
Core README documents CircuitBreakerOptions and adaptive retry config
Cluster scope limitation (per-process only) documented clearly

findings: IConsumerClient has no PauseAsync/ResumeAsync — must be added to interface and 8 transport implementations
ConsumerRegister uses single shared CancellationTokenSource for all groups — must refactor to per-group CTS
ConsumerRegister._isHealthy is a single global volatile bool — circuit breaker needs per-group state via separate ICircuitBreakerStateManager
IConsumeFilter is single-instance (GetService<IConsumeFilter>), not a pipeline — cannot use as circuit breaker owner
MessageNeedToRetryProcessor uses fixed _waitingInterval with no adaptive behavior
ExponentialBackoffStrategy.ShouldRetry classifies exceptions but is insufficient for circuit breaker (NullReferenceException returns true)
SubscribeExecutor swallows OperationCanceledException unconditionally — HttpClient timeouts are invisible
TransportCheckProcessor polls _isHealthy every 30s and calls ReStartAsync — must not reset circuit state
